name: Read-Host

options.name: -AsSecureString
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet displays asterisks (`*`) in place of the characters that the user types as input. When you use this parameter, the output of the `Read-Host` cmdlet is a SecureString object ( System.Security.SecureString ).

options.name: -MaskInput
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet displays asterisks (`*`) in place of the characters that the user types as input. When you use this parameter, the output of the `Read-Host` cmdlet is a String object. This allows you to safely prompt for a password that is returned as plaintext instead of SecureString .

options.name: -Prompt
options.description: Specifies the text of the prompt. Type a string. If the string includes spaces, enclose it in quotation marks. PowerShell appends a colon (`:`) to the text that you enter.

args.name: -Prompt
args.description: Specifies the text of the prompt. Type a string. If the string includes spaces, enclose it in quotation marks. PowerShell appends a colon (`:`) to the text that you enter.

description: Reads a line of input from the console.
